display.purpose: Hero headlines on public pages only. Capped at clamp(2.5rem, 6vw, 6rem).

body.purpose: Paragraph copy, table cells, form helper text.

label.purpose: Nav links, buttons, badges, table headers, stat-card eyebrows — the font-accent utility.

mono.purpose: Token codes, masked credential fields, JSON/embed-code snippets only — never headings, labels, or body copy.

shadows.name: cta-glow
shadows.purpose: Primary CTA buttons and the hero card only — signals 'hottest element,' not elevation.

shadows.name: nav-scrim
shadows.purpose: Sticky nav bar over scrolling content. Only sanctioned blur usage.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default ease-out-quart for hover/transition states (cards, nav underline, buttons).

motion.name: duration-standard
motion.purpose: Transform transitions (card lift, nav underline scale).

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one primary action per screen — hero CTA, form submit.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #007AFF; color: #FFFFFF; padding: 16px 32px; border-radius: 6px; border: none; font-family: 'Barlow Condensed', sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 13px; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; transition: background 0.2s ease; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #0D6EFD; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0081C6; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Outline button — used wherever azure-cta is already taken on screen.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: transparent; color: #0081C6; padding: 14px 30px; border-radius: 6px; border: 2px solid #0081C6; font-family: 'Barlow Condensed', sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 13px; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; transition: background 0.2s ease, color 0.2s ease; cursor: pointer; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #0081C6; color: #002359; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #0081C6; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Pending/approved/rejected/live pill — color is the only variant axis.
components.html: Pendiente
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; padding: 4px 10px; border-radius: 9999px; font-family: 'Barlow Condensed', sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 12px; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; border-width: 1px; border-style: solid; } .ds-badge--warning { background: rgb(251 191 36 / 0.2); color: #FBBF24; border-color: rgb(251 191 36 / 0.3); } .ds-badge--success { background: rgb(34 197 94 / 0.2); color: #22C55E; border-color: rgb(34 197 94 / 0.3); } .ds-badge--error { background: rgb(239 68 68 / 0.2); color: #EF4444; border-color: rgb(239 68 68 / 0.3); }

components.name: Stat Card
components.description: Admin dashboard stat tile — flat, border-led, no shadow at rest.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #002359; border: 1px solid #004F8E; border-radius: 12px; padding: 20px; transition: border-color 0.25s ease, transform 0.25s ease; } .ds-card:hover { border-color: #0081C6; transform: translateY(-4px); } .ds-card__label { font-family: 'Barlow Condensed', sans-serif; font-size: 12px; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #AAAAAA; margin-bottom: 6px; } .ds-card__value { font-family: 'Oswald', sans-serif; font-size: 28px; font-weight: 700; color: #EEEEEE; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard form field across admin and public registration forms.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; background: #003873; border: 1px solid #004F8E; border-radius: 6px; padding: 10px 12px; color: #EEEEEE; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; transition: border-color 0.2s ease; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #AAAAAA; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #0081C6; }

components.name: Live Status Indicator
components.description: Pulsing dot for 'recently updated' ranking callouts — must be paired with cached-as-of copy, never claim real-time live scoring.
components.css: .ds-live-dot { display: inline-block; width: 10px; height: 10px; border-radius: 9999px; background: #22C55E; box-shadow: 0 0 0 0 rgb(34 197 94 / 0.7); animation: ds-pulse-live 1.6s infinite; } @keyframes ds-pulse-live { 0% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 0 rgb(34 197 94 / 0.6); } 70% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 10px rgb(34 197 94 / 0); } 100% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 0 rgb(34 197 94 / 0); } } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-live-dot { animation: none; } }

narrative.overview:
This system reads like the on-screen graphics package for a live international surf broadcast, not a generic web app. The base is a stacked navy field — navy-deepest → navy-dark → navy-mid — that holds the screen the way a broadcast lower-third holds the bottom of a frame: dark, stable, never competing for attention. Condensed, uppercase Barlow Condensed labels and Oswald display headlines carry the authority of a results board; the sky-cyan and azure-cta accents do the work that broadcast graphics use a single hot color for — live indicators, the one action that matters, the number that just changed.

The system explicitly rejects the cream/card-grid SaaS dashboard look and the cluttered, low-density federation-site look this platform replaces (see PRODUCT.md anti-references). It is built for two postures from one palette: the public surfaces broadcast the tour outward (bigger type, motion, gradient hero treatment), while the admin/competitor console runs the tour internally (denser, border-led, fewer flourishes) — but both pull from the exact same navy/azure/cyan tokens, never a second palette.
keyCharacteristics: Navy-on-navy layering (3 steps of the same hue) instead of light/dark contrast for surface hierarchy
One hot accent (azure-cta, #007AFF) reserved for the single primary action per screen
Condensed, uppercase, tracked labels (font-accent / Barlow Condensed) for anything chip-, nav-, or status-shaped
Flat surfaces, 1px borders for separation — shadows are rare and reserved for accent glows, not structure
Status meaning (success/warning/error/live) always carried by a named color token, never decoration alone

rules.name: The Honest Naming Rule
rules.body: The live Tailwind config still names a token orange-brand even though it resolves to #007AFF (actually azure) — leftover naming from an earlier orange accent swapped to blue and never renamed. (gold-brand, which duplicated cyan-brand exactly, has already been consolidated.) Document true colors under honest names; treat the orange-brand/orange-light rename as an outstanding polish item.

rules.name: The One Hot Color Rule
rules.body: azure-cta appears on exactly one element class of action per screen. If two buttons fight for azure-cta, one is wrong — demote it to sky-cyan outline or ghost.

rules.name: The Status-Never-Alone Rule
rules.body: Success/warning/error meaning is never carried by color alone — always pair with an icon, label, or shape.

rules.name: The One Voice Per Element Rule
rules.body: Headings are always Oswald, body is always Inter, anything uppercase-and-tracked is always Barlow Condensed. Never mix.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces sit flush against their navy layer. Reach for a border before reaching for a shadow. Reserve shadow/glow for the single hottest element on screen.

dos: Do keep the navy three-step layering as the only depth mechanism on a given screen
Do reserve azure-cta (#007AFF) for exactly one primary action per screen
Do set anything chip-, nav-, or label-shaped in Barlow Condensed, uppercase, 0.08em tracking
Do pair every status color with an icon or label, never color alone
Do keep 'Results by SurfScores.com' visible and linked on every screen showing scores or rankings
Do cap display headings at clamp(2.5rem, 6vw, 6rem)
Do rename the drifted orange-brand/orange-light Tailwind tokens to their true azure value as a future polish cleanup
Do use 'ALAS Latin Tour' consistently across every page title
donts: Don't introduce a second brand hue family — status colors are the only departure from navy/azure/cyan
Don't use rounded-full on anything but badges/pills/avatars/dots
Don't add box-shadows for structural elevation
Don't use cream/card-grid SaaS dashboard patterns, hero-metric templates, tiny uppercase eyebrows, nested cards, or side-stripe colored borders
Don't ship the cluttered, dense, unstyled-table look of the legacy ASP-era site
Don't mix font families across the same element type
Don't imply real-time live scoring anywhere
